pageFaqs.ctaBody: Send us your spec, drawing or reference sample — our sales team replies with a real quote within one business day.
pageFaqs.aboutIntro: Common questions about our factory, history and how to verify our credentials.
pageFaqs.aboutQ1: How long has CHIC been manufacturing wooden products?
pageFaqs.aboutA1: CHIC's factory was established in 2017 in Cao County, Shandong — one of China's most established wooden-products manufacturing clusters. Our export entity, Xiamen Chic Homeware Co., Ltd., was registered in 2020 to handle direct B2B export. Combined, our leadership team brings more than a decade of hands-on wooden manufacturing experience.
pageFaqs.aboutA2: Our 20,000 m² production base is in Pulianji Town, Cao County, Heze City, Shandong Province — a region recognised as one of China's leading wooden-products clusters. Our sales and export office is in Xinglin, Jimei District, Xiamen, with direct access to Xiamen Port for international shipping.
pageFaqs.aboutQ3: How many countries does CHIC export to?
pageFaqs.aboutA3: We currently ship to more than 60 countries across North America (US, Canada), Europe (UK, Germany, France, Spain, Italy, the Netherlands), Asia-Pacific (Japan, Korea, Australia, New Zealand) and select Middle East markets. The largest concentrations of long-term customers are in the US, UK, Germany, Japan, Canada and Australia.
pageFaqs.aboutQ4: Is CHIC a manufacturer or a trading company?
pageFaqs.aboutA4: CHIC is a full manufacturer — we own and operate our own 20,000 m² production facility in Cao County, Shandong, with around 120 skilled workers. The Xiamen Chic Homeware Co., Ltd. trading entity is part of the same company, set up specifically to handle export documentation, FBA logistics and direct B2B contracts.
pageFaqs.aboutQ5: Who runs CHIC day to day?
pageFaqs.aboutA5: CHIC is founder-led — Chuan Pu set up the factory in 2017 and remains hands-on with material and design decisions. The leadership team includes a dedicated head of sales (Cassie Zhang), production manager (Yucheng Lu), design lead (Vivi Cheng) and head of quality (Lingling Zhang). Their full profiles live on the Meet the Team page.
pageFaqs.aboutQ6: Are you an Alibaba Gold Supplier?
pageFaqs.aboutA6: Yes — CHIC was verified as an Alibaba Gold Supplier in 2025. Our verified profile is at quke.en.alibaba.com. Buyers who prefer to begin the relationship through Alibaba's transaction protections are welcome to start there, then move to direct contract for larger orders.
pageFaqs.aboutA7: Yes. Buyers from the US, EU, Japan, Korea, Australia and the Middle East have visited both our Xiamen office and our Cao County production base. Please book at least two weeks in advance so we can confirm production schedules, prepare samples relevant to your project and arrange a translator if required.
pageFaqs.teamIntro: How you'll work with us — who does what, languages, and how communication is handled.
pageFaqs.teamQ1: Can I work directly with the same person throughout my project?
pageFaqs.teamA1: Yes. Each B2B account is assigned a dedicated sales contact (usually Cassie Zhang or a senior account manager) who stays with you from first inquiry through sampling, production, shipping and reorders. You won't be passed between random team members between emails.
pageFaqs.teamA2: Our sales team communicates fluently in English, Mandarin Chinese and Japanese. We've handled inquiries in Spanish, German and French through written translation — for ongoing complex projects in those languages we recommend pairing with a local trade agent, which we're happy to coordinate.
pageFaqs.teamQ3: Who do I contact if I have a quality issue mid-production?
pageFaqs.teamA3: Quality issues are escalated directly to Lingling Zhang, our head of quality. She reviews the case, walks the floor with production, and reports back within one business day with photos, root cause and a corrective-action proposal. No issue is hidden or quietly shipped.
pageFaqs.teamQ4: Can I speak directly with the founder?
pageFaqs.teamA4: Yes — for strategic decisions (new product line, large multi-year programs, sustainability roadmaps) Chuan Pu personally joins calls. Day-to-day operational matters go through your assigned sales contact so the founder's time stays focused on direction-setting work.
pageFaqs.teamA5: Sales and design: Monday–Saturday, 9:00–18:00 GMT+8 (China time). Factory production: Monday–Saturday, 7:30–17:30 GMT+8. Email and WhatsApp inquiries outside hours are normally answered within one business day; urgent issues during peak season are monitored on Sunday as well.
pageFaqs.capabilitiesIntro: Production capacity, equipment, tolerances and QC — the technical questions buyers usually ask before sampling.
pageFaqs.capabilitiesQ1: What is CHIC's monthly production capacity?
pageFaqs.capabilitiesA1: Our 20,000 m² facility produces approximately 100,000–150,000 wooden pieces per month across all product lines (boxes, trays, organisers, kitchenware). Capacity scales with SKU complexity — simple bamboo trays run faster than multi-component watch boxes with hardware, so exact lead times are quoted per project.
pageFaqs.capabilitiesA2: We run CNC routing for precision cuts, laser engraving and laser cutting for logos and small parts, screen printing and UV printing for surface graphics, hot-foil stamping for premium branding, kiln-drying ovens for moisture control, and dedicated finishing lines for oiling, lacquering, staining and painting.
pageFaqs.capabilitiesQ3: Can you make products that don't exist in your catalog?
pageFaqs.capabilitiesA3: Yes — about 70% of our annual production is custom OEM. Send a sample, a sketch, a CAD file or just a description of what you need, and our design lead will come back with a structural feasibility review and material / finish options within three to five business days.
pageFaqs.capabilitiesA4: Three checkpoints: (1) incoming material inspection — moisture content, grain consistency, defect rejection; (2) in-process check at sanding and finishing stages; (3) pre-shipment inspection — AQL 2.5 sampling on standard orders, and 100% visual inspection on premium accounts and Japan-bound shipments. Full QC reports with photos are available on request.
pageFaqs.capabilitiesQ5: How tight can you hold dimensional tolerances?
pageFaqs.capabilitiesA5: Standard wood-product tolerances are ±1 mm on cut dimensions and ±0.5 mm on milled features. For fitted parts (e.g., box-and-lid interfaces, hinge mounts, magnetic closures) we hold ±0.3 mm. Tighter tolerances are possible on request but typically increase reject rate and unit cost.
pageFaqs.capabilitiesA6: Yes — hinges, magnetic closures, metal latches, brass corner caps, leather and fabric lining, foam inserts, EVA cutouts and ribbon pulls can all be installed in-house. For repeat OEM programs we hold consigned hardware so production can start without waiting on each shipment of metal parts.
pageFaqs.capabilitiesQ7: Can you do small first runs to test the market?
pageFaqs.capabilitiesA7: Yes. While our standard MOQ is 300–500 pcs per design, we accept lower trial runs (100–200 pcs) for new B2B partners — typically at a slightly higher unit price that reflects the per-set-up cost. This protects you from a large bet on an unproven SKU.
pageFaqs.woodFabricationIntro: How we prepare, shape, join and finish wood before it ships.
pageFaqs.woodFabricationQ1: How does CHIC dry its wood before production?
pageFaqs.woodFabricationA1: All wood is kiln-dried to 8–12% moisture content (the standard range for export-bound finished products) and held at that level until production starts. We monitor moisture daily and apply a final dehumidification adjusted to the destination market — drier for Middle East shipments, slightly higher for tropical-climate destinations.
pageFaqs.woodFabricationQ2: What joinery methods can you produce?
pageFaqs.woodFabricationA2: Butt joints with glue + dowel (highest volume), finger joints for visible decorative joinery, miter joints with biscuit reinforcement, full dovetail joinery for premium box programs, and tongue-and-groove for cabinet-style pieces. Joinery method is chosen based on stress points, visible surfaces and the buyer's price target.
pageFaqs.woodFabricationA3: Natural mineral or food-grade oil for cutting boards and kitchenware; matte, satin or gloss water-based lacquer; alcohol-based stains (light, medium, dark or custom Pantone); painted finishes; and distressed or rustic looks. All food-contact finishes are aligned with FDA and LFGB standards and can be third-party tested per order.
pageFaqs.woodFabricationA4: Yes. Bamboo laser-engraves cleanly with deep contrast — popular for branding spice racks, cutting boards, tea boxes and serving trays. Engraving depth is adjustable from a light surface mark (0.1 mm) to deep relief (1 mm or more), depending on whether the logo is purely visual or has a tactile feel.
pageFaqs.woodFabricationQ5: How do you prevent wood from cracking during export shipping?
pageFaqs.woodFabricationA5: Three controls: (1) moisture target adjusted to destination climate at the drying stage; (2) inner cartons with corner protection and silica-gel desiccants for long-haul ocean freight; (3) export-grade outer cartons rated through drop-test simulation. Reported after-shipping crack rate across our long-term accounts is typically under 0.5%.
pageFaqs.woodFabricationQ6: What's the difference between bamboo and acacia for kitchenware?
pageFaqs.woodFabricationA6: Bamboo is lighter, faster-regrowing, naturally antimicrobial and 20–30% cheaper at retail. Acacia is heavier, has richer tiger-stripe grain and commands a 30–50% retail premium. Our material guide has a full side-by-side comparison covering hardness, water resistance, sustainability and ideal applications.
pageFaqs.productsIntro: How to request samples, get a quote, and move from first email to bulk shipment.
pageFaqs.productsA1: Submit our contact form with the product(s) you're interested in plus your shipping address. Sample charges depend on the product (typically USD 20–80 per piece) and are normally credited back against your first bulk order. Sample lead time is 7–10 days; courier (FedEx, DHL, UPS) is charged to your account.
pageFaqs.productsQ2: Do you have a printed catalog?
pageFaqs.productsA2: We don't print a glossy retail-style catalog because our line is custom-OEM heavy and changes frequently. We do provide PDF catalogs by category on request, and we can build a customer-specific catalog (your logo, your suggested SKUs, your target FOB prices) within a few business days.
pageFaqs.productsA3: Each quote is built from: material cost (varies by wood type and grade), processing complexity (CNC time, hand-finishing steps, hardware), packaging (color box, kraft sleeve, FBA-ready master cartons), QC level required, and quantity tier. We send fully itemised quotes so you can see exactly where the money goes.
pageFaqs.productsQ4: What is the typical sample-to-bulk timeline?
pageFaqs.productsA4: Sample: 7–10 days. Sample review and revision: usually one or two rounds, 10–14 days total. Bulk production after sample approval: 25–35 days. So from first inquiry to FOB-ready bulk: about 8–10 weeks for a new SKU; faster (3–5 weeks) on repeat orders of an already-approved design.
pageFaqs.productsQ5: Can I order existing catalog SKUs without customization?
pageFaqs.productsA5: Yes — about 30% of our orders are catalog SKUs with minimal modification (your logo plus your packaging). MOQ on catalog SKUs is typically lower (100–300 pcs) than full custom (300–500 pcs), and lead times are shorter because tooling and finish recipes are already proven.
pageFaqs.productsQ6: Do you ship directly to Amazon FBA warehouses?
pageFaqs.productsA6: Yes. We have established workflows for FBA-ready packaging — barcode labels per Amazon spec, master carton marks, drop-test simulation. We've shipped to FBA warehouses in the US, UK, Canada, Germany, France, Italy, Spain, Japan and Australia, so customs paperwork for those routes is dialled in.
pageFaqs.productsQ7: What payment terms do you accept?
pageFaqs.productsA7: Standard terms: 30% deposit on order confirmation, 70% balance against B/L copy before shipment. T/T, PayPal (up to certain thresholds), Alibaba Trade Assurance and L/C at sight are all supported. For long-term partners with a track record we offer net-30 on a portion of the balance.
pageFaqs.blogIntro: Useful answers from the team for sourcing managers and brand owners researching wooden products.
pageFaqs.blogQ1: How often does CHIC publish new content?
pageFaqs.blogA1: We publish one or two new articles per month, written by our sales and design leadership based on real questions we get from B2B buyers. Topics rotate between material guides, sourcing checklists, OEM process explainers and market trend analysis for kitchenware, gift packaging and home organisation.
pageFaqs.blogQ2: Can I republish CHIC's blog content on my own site?
pageFaqs.blogA2: Short excerpts (under 100 words) with a link back to the original article are welcome. Full-article republication requires written permission — please email info@xmchichomeware.com so we can confirm and provide a clean attribution block. We're often happy to grant it for trade publications and retailer education sites.
pageFaqs.blogQ3: Do you take guest posts or paid links?
pageFaqs.blogA3: No. We don't accept paid placements, sponsored posts or backlink exchanges. All articles are written by our internal team to share what we've learned operating a wooden-products factory. If you'd like to collaborate on a co-authored case study based on a real CHIC OEM project, please contact us directly.
pageFaqs.blogQ4: How do I get notified about new articles?
pageFaqs.blogA4: Subscribe to the newsletter in the site footer — about one email per month, no spam, unsubscribe in one click. We send a brief digest of new articles, plus occasional updates on production calendar (peak-season cut-offs, holiday closures) that affect B2B buyers.
pageFaqs.blogQ5: Can I suggest an article topic?
pageFaqs.blogA5: Yes — we actively take suggestions from B2B buyers. Email info@xmchichomeware.com with the topic and a bit of context on what you're trying to figure out. If it's a question multiple buyers are asking, it usually becomes an article within a month or two.

category.defaultIntro: Common questions about sourcing this product category from CHIC's factory.
category.defaultQ1: What is the typical MOQ for this product category?
category.defaultA1: Standard MOQ is 300–500 pcs per design; lower trial runs of 100–200 pcs are accepted for new B2B partners at a slightly higher unit price. MOQ varies by complexity — simple bamboo pieces run lower, multi-component hardware items run higher.
category.defaultQ2: What lead time should I plan for?
category.defaultA2: Sample: 7–10 days. Bulk production: 25–35 days after sample approval. For a brand-new SKU, plan 8–10 weeks from first inquiry to FOB-ready bulk; repeat orders of approved designs ship in 3–5 weeks.
category.defaultQ3: What custom branding options are available?
category.defaultA3: Laser engraving, hot-foil stamping, screen printing, UV printing, debossed branding, custom-printed inserts and full custom color-box packaging are all available. We help match the branding method to the wood type and your target retail positioning.
category.defaultQ4: Which wood materials work best for this category?
category.defaultA4: Bamboo, acacia, walnut, pine, paulownia, oak and beech all have their place — material is chosen based on your target retail price, durability requirements and finish look. Our material guide compares all twelve woods we work with side by side.
category.defaultA5: Yes — barcode labels per Amazon spec, master carton marks and drop-test-aware packaging are standard on FBA-bound orders. We've shipped to FBA warehouses in the US, UK, Canada, Germany, France, Italy, Spain, Japan and Australia.
category.cheeseBoardIntro: Sourcing cheese boards and charcuterie platters for retail, gifting and hospitality.
category.cheeseBoardQ1: What size is most popular for retail cheese boards?
category.cheeseBoardA1: Two sizes cover roughly 80% of retail demand: a single-serving 25×15 cm board (around 10×6 inches) for gift sets and small charcuterie, and a 40×30 cm board (around 16×12 inches) for dinner-party retail. Larger 50–60 cm presentation boards are sold mostly into hospitality.
category.cheeseBoardQ2: Which woods are food-safe for cheese boards?
category.cheeseBoardA2: Acacia, walnut, beech, bamboo, teak and oak are all food-safe when finished with FDA / LFGB-compliant food-grade oil or mineral oil. Acacia is the most popular for premium retail because of its rich grain; bamboo wins on price and eco positioning.
category.cheeseBoardQ3: Can you include a matching knife or cheese-tool set?
category.cheeseBoardA3: Yes — stainless steel knife sets, slide-out cutlery drawers, ceramic spreaders and slate insert plates are all available as bundled OEM. We coordinate the hardware supplier and pack everything as a complete retail-ready set.
category.cheeseBoardQ4: Do you offer juice-groove cheese boards?
category.cheeseBoardA4: Yes. Juice grooves are routed around the perimeter to catch liquids — popular on cutting-board-style cheese boards for hard cheeses and meats. Depth and groove width are customisable; we recommend 5 mm deep × 8 mm wide as a standard.
category.cheeseBoardQ5: What's the right finish for daily-use cheese boards?
category.cheeseBoardA5: Food-grade mineral oil or beeswax — easy to re-apply at home, no chemical residue, and brings out the grain. Avoid heavy lacquer for daily cutting use, as it can chip under knife pressure; lacquer is fine for display-only charcuterie pieces.
category.cuttingBoardIntro: Sourcing wooden cutting boards for retail, restaurants, and branded gift programs.
category.cuttingBoardQ1: End-grain vs edge-grain — which should I source?
category.cuttingBoardA1: End-grain boards (where the fibres run vertically) are easier on knife edges and self-heal microcuts, but cost 30–50% more. Edge-grain boards are the retail standard — durable, less expensive, and fine for everyday home use. End-grain makes sense for premium butcher-block programs.
category.cuttingBoardQ2: Which woods should I avoid for cutting boards?
category.cuttingBoardA2: Avoid soft woods (pine, paulownia) for knife use — they cut, splinter and trap moisture. Avoid open-pore woods like oak for raw-protein prep, as the pores trap juices. Best choices: hard maple, beech, walnut, acacia and bamboo (technically a grass, but behaves like a hardwood here).
category.cuttingBoardQ3: Do you supply juice grooves and handles?
category.cuttingBoardA3: Yes — juice grooves (perimeter or one-side), built-in carry handles, rope-loop hangers and angled finger lifts can all be customised. Tell us how the buyer plans to use the board and we'll suggest the right combination.
category.cuttingBoardA4: No — wooden cutting boards should always be hand-washed. Repeated dishwasher cycles warp the wood and strip the finish. We include a 'hand wash only' care card with retail packaging on request, plus monthly mineral-oil maintenance instructions.
category.cuttingBoardQ5: Can you brand the back side with the buyer's logo?
category.cuttingBoardA5: Yes. Laser engraving on the underside is the most common option — clean, permanent, and doesn't interfere with the cutting surface. Hot-foil stamping and printed labels are also possible if a more decorative look is needed.
category.servingTrayIntro: Wooden serving trays — for breakfast in bed, ottoman service, hospitality and retail gift lines.
category.servingTrayQ1: What sizes are most popular for serving trays?
category.servingTrayQ2: What's the right finish for a serving tray used with hot dishes?
category.servingTrayA1: Small (30×20 cm) for breakfast and beverage service, medium (40×30 cm) for full meal service, large ottoman trays (60×40 cm) for living-room serving. Sofa trays follow the same widths but with longer arms that extend over couch cushions.
category.servingTrayA2: A heat-resistant lacquer or a thicker oil-and-wax build. Avoid raw natural oil only — hot mugs can leave white rings on lightly oiled wood. For hospitality, we recommend a satin water-based lacquer rated for temperatures up to 90°C.
category.servingTrayQ3: Do you make trays with built-in carry handles?
category.servingTrayA3: Yes — cut-out side handles, rope handles, leather strap handles and metal D-ring handles are all available. The handle style is usually picked to match the rest of the buyer's product line aesthetic (modern, rustic, hospitality, etc.).
category.servingTrayA4: Yes. We support custom size, handle style, edge profile and finish. For sofa / bed trays the most common adjustment is the extended-arm length to fit specific couch widths — common ranges are 70–90 cm and 90–110 cm.
category.servingTrayQ5: What's the difference between a sofa tray and an ottoman tray?
category.servingTrayA5: A sofa tray has extending arms that rest on the cushions of a couch — designed to hover over your lap. An ottoman tray has a flat base and sits on top of an ottoman or coffee table. We make both styles, and several SKUs that convert between the two.
category.spiceRackIntro: Wooden spice racks and full spice-jar sets for kitchen brands, Amazon sellers and home-goods retail.
category.spiceRackQ1: Do you supply matching glass jars with the rack?
category.spiceRackA1: Yes — square, round or hexagonal glass jars in 80 ml, 100 ml, 120 ml and 150 ml capacities, with wood, bamboo, metal or plastic lids. We also include labels, funnels and shaker-top inserts on request. Full kitchen-organiser kits ship as a complete retail-ready set.
category.spiceRackQ2: Countertop vs wall-mounted — which sells better?
category.spiceRackA2: Countertop racks (free-standing, often two-tier) sell better in Amazon and direct-to-consumer channels. Wall-mounted racks sell better through home-organisation retail and influencer programs. Many of our buyers run both as different SKUs in the same range.
category.spiceRackQ3: Can you do drawer-insert spice organisers?
category.spiceRackA3: Yes — angled drawer-insert spice organisers (5°–10° tilt so labels are visible from above) are increasingly popular in US and EU kitchen-organisation retail. We supply them as standalone organisers or as part of a full drawer-insert set with cutlery and prep tools.
category.spiceRackQ4: How many jars should fit in a standard rack?
category.spiceRackA4: Common configurations are 6, 8, 10, 12 and 16 jars. Twelve is the most popular for retail kitchenware brands — enough to cover everyday spices without feeling sparse. Sixteen-jar racks tend to be flagship SKUs at the top of a brand's ladder.
category.spiceRackQ5: Do you offer branded jar labels?
category.spiceRackA5: Yes. Printed sticker labels, blank labels for the end-user to fill in, magnetic lid labels and engraved wooden lids are all options. For full-set retail programs we coordinate label design with your brand guidelines so the typography matches the rest of your product range.
category.storageBoxIntro: Wooden storage boxes — for closet, vanity, pantry, craft and branded home-organisation lines.
category.storageBoxQ1: Can I customise the box interior with dividers and inserts?
category.storageBoxA1: Yes — EVA foam, velvet wrap, microfibre lining, molded pulp inserts, fabric drawstring liners and custom-cut dividers cut to your product dimensions are all available. Insert design is one of the highest-impact ways to differentiate a wooden box from competitors.
category.storageBoxQ2: Hinged lid vs sliding lid — which works better?
category.storageBoxA2: Hinged lids feel more premium and unbox better for gift programs. Sliding lids stack better in storage and are more child-resistant. We make both — choice usually comes down to whether the box is positioned as gift-first or function-first.
category.storageBoxQ3: What stacking strength can wooden storage boxes hold?
category.storageBoxA3: Properly engineered solid-wood and plywood-frame boxes can stack 3–5 high under normal household weight. We do drop-test and stack-load simulation on first samples; if your use case is industrial storage or warehousing we can over-engineer accordingly.
category.storageBoxQ4: Can you do nesting box sets?
category.storageBoxA4: Yes. Three-piece, four-piece and five-piece nesting sets (each box slightly smaller, fitting inside the next) are popular in retail home-organisation. We coordinate the dimensional cascade so nothing rattles or sticks, even with humidity changes during shipping.
category.storageBoxQ5: What's the typical finish for closet / vanity storage?
category.storageBoxA5: Soft matte lacquer in white, beige, sage or natural wood tones — these match the neutral-palette home decor that dominates US and EU home-organisation retail. Distressed or rustic finishes also sell well for farmhouse-positioned brands.
category.jewelryBoxIntro: Wooden jewelry, watch and ring boxes — for brand packaging, boutique retail and proposal gifts.
category.jewelryBoxA1: Velvet (cream, navy, black, burgundy, custom Pantone), microfibre, satin, EVA foam and custom-cut foam inserts. For premium watch boxes we also offer leather and faux-leather wrapped interiors. Linings are cut and fitted in-house so seams are clean.
category.jewelryBoxQ2: Can you make custom-shape ring slots, watch pillows and necklace hooks?
category.jewelryBoxA2: Yes — ring slots are CNC-cut and lined; watch pillows are foam-cored and fabric-wrapped to your watch dimensions; necklace hooks can be metal, fabric loop or magnetic. Send us your jewelry dimensions and we'll produce a fitted insert.
category.jewelryBoxQ3: What hardware do you offer for premium jewelry boxes?
category.jewelryBoxA3: Concealed hinges, gold or silver-tone clasps, magnetic closures, lock-and-key mechanisms, and decorative metal corner caps. We have established supply relationships for all of these, so production doesn't wait on hardware sourcing.
category.jewelryBoxQ4: Can you supply a glass-top viewing lid?
category.jewelryBoxA4: Yes. Glass-top jewelry and watch boxes are popular for retail display. We use tempered glass with mitered wood frames, and can add UV-blocking glass on request to prevent fabric fade on long-term display pieces.
category.jewelryBoxQ5: Do you make ring boxes for proposal use?
category.jewelryBoxA5: Yes — slim single-ring, double-ring, and 'his-and-hers' ring box designs are all in our line. Walnut and acacia in classic silhouettes are most popular for proposal gifts; we also do custom monogram engraving for personalised pieces.
category.wineBoxIntro: Wooden wine and spirits boxes — single-bottle, multi-bottle, gift and corporate packaging.
category.wineBoxQ1: Can you fit wine boxes to specific bottle shapes?
category.wineBoxA1: Yes — we custom-cut bottle compartments for Bordeaux, Burgundy, Champagne, Riesling, magnums, miniatures and bespoke craft-spirit bottles. Send a bottle sample or its dimensions and label height, and we build the interior around it.
category.wineBoxQ2: Are sliding-lid or hinged-lid wine boxes more popular?
category.wineBoxA2: Sliding-lid is the traditional pine wine-crate look and remains popular for entry to mid-tier gifting. Hinged-lid with a magnetic or clasp closure feels more premium and sells better at higher retail tiers. Both are easy to brand with logo engraving or hot foil.
category.wineBoxQ3: Can you include glasses, openers or accessories inside the wine box?
category.wineBoxA3: Yes. We coordinate glassware, corkscrews, drip stoppers, decanters and tasting cards as bundled OEM. The full gift-set version sits one or two retail tiers above a bottle-only box and is a very popular B2B program for Q4 holiday gifting.
category.wineBoxQ4: What woods look best for wine boxes?
category.wineBoxA4: Pine for rustic / traditional, paulownia for lighter-weight gift programs, walnut for premium presentation, acacia for the in-between premium look, and MDF veneer when consistency and painted finishes matter. Most buyers run pine for entry tier and walnut for premium.
category.wineBoxQ5: Can you do double-bottle and tasting-set boxes?
category.wineBoxA5: Yes — double-bottle horizontal layouts, tasting flights of 4–6 bottles, and miniature tasting sets are all in-line. Internal dividers and bottle restraints are sized to your specific bottles so nothing rattles during shipping.
category.teaBoxIntro: Wooden tea boxes and tea-bag organisers — for loose-leaf brands, cafés, and gift programs.
category.teaBoxQ1: How many compartments do tea boxes usually have?
category.teaBoxA1: Four, six, eight and twelve compartments are the standard configurations. Eight is the most popular for retail loose-leaf brands; twelve makes sense for premium retail and tasting-set programs. We also do custom layouts (e.g., three compartments with one large central tray for accessories).
category.teaBoxQ2: Can you make tea boxes with glass viewing lids?
category.teaBoxA2: Yes — glass-lid tea boxes are a popular premium option that shows off the blend selection while keeping the tea fresh. We use mitered wood frames with tempered glass; lid can be hinged or removable depending on the look you want.
category.teaBoxA3: Yes. Tea boxes are finished with food-grade mineral oil or food-safe lacquer, and we test for migration to standards aligned with FDA and LFGB. The compartments hold sealed tea bags or loose leaf in food-grade liners, so there's no direct wood-to-loose-leaf contact required.
category.teaBoxQ4: Can you brand each compartment with the tea variety?
category.teaBoxA4: Yes — engraved labels on each compartment, printed sticker labels or magnetic interchangeable name tags are all options. Magnetic tags are useful for cafés that rotate tea selections through the same display box.
category.teaBoxQ5: Do you offer matching loose-leaf accessories?
category.teaBoxA5: Yes — wooden tea scoops, bamboo tea trays, glass jars for bulk loose-leaf storage and wooden coasters. The full 'tea ritual' product line is a popular Q4 holiday gift program for tea brands.
category.giftBoxIntro: Wooden gift and keepsake boxes — corporate, seasonal, retail and subscription packaging.
category.giftBoxQ1: Can you handle holiday-season volume spikes?
category.giftBoxA1: Yes. We typically produce 50,000+ gift boxes monthly during Q4. Book your order by August for guaranteed Q4 delivery — by September it's tight, and by October we usually have to negotiate which boxes ship in which week. Our Canadian, US and EU partners book early for this reason.
category.giftBoxQ2: What's the difference between a gift box and a keepsake box?
category.giftBoxA2: A gift box is designed for one-time use as packaging — usually printed branding, slimmer wood, sometimes a single-use ribbon. A keepsake box is designed to be retained and reused after the gift is opened — heavier wood, hinged lid, longer-lasting branding (engraving instead of print).
category.giftBoxQ3: Can you do custom shapes and unusual sizes?
category.giftBoxA3: Yes — heart-shaped, hexagonal, oval, octagonal and custom contour-cut boxes are all producible with CNC routing. Custom shapes typically push MOQ up and unit price up, but for premium gift programs they're worth it for the differentiation.
category.giftBoxA4: We supply several subscription-box brands — same wooden box reused monthly with rotating contents, or a 'graduating' set where each month's box stacks or nests with the previous. The reusability angle is a key selling point for sustainability-positioned subscription brands.
category.giftBoxQ5: Can you include printed inserts and care cards?
category.giftBoxA5: Yes — coated-paper or kraft-paper inserts, instruction cards, brand-story cards and care guides can all be printed and co-packed inside the box. This is one of the easiest ways to upgrade perceived value without changing the wooden box itself.

privacy.metaDesc: How Xiamen Chic Homeware (CHIC) collects, uses, and protects your personal data when you contact us or browse our B2B wooden products site.
privacy.translationNote: This translation is provided for your convenience. In case of any discrepancy, the English version of this Privacy Policy prevails.
privacy.intro: Xiamen Chic Homeware Co., Ltd. ("CHIC", "we", "us", "our") respects your privacy. This Privacy Policy explains what personal information we collect, how we use it, and the choices and rights you have. It applies to xmchichomeware.com and all our online communications with you (contact forms, email, newsletter).
privacy.s1Body: We are Xiamen Chic Homeware Co., Ltd., a wooden products manufacturer based in Xiamen, Fujian, China, with a production facility in Heze, Shandong. We are the data controller for the personal information described in this policy. You can reach our privacy team at info@xmchichomeware.com.
privacy.s2Intro: We collect the following categories of personal information:
privacy.s2L1: Contact form submissions — name, email address, company name, message, country/region, and any technical specifications you choose to share.
privacy.s2L3: Business correspondence — email exchanges, WhatsApp messages, and other communications with our sales team.
privacy.s2L4: Automatically collected technical data — IP address (anonymized by our analytics tools), browser type, operating system, device type, referring URL, pages visited, time on page, and click behavior.
privacy.s2L5: Session recordings — Microsoft Clarity may record anonymized recordings of how you interact with our site (mouse movement, clicks, scroll behavior). Sensitive form fields are automatically masked.
privacy.s3Intro: We use your information for the following purposes:
privacy.s3L1: Respond to your inquiries and provide quotes for our products and services.
privacy.s3L2: Fulfill orders, manage customer relationships, and coordinate production, shipping, and after-sales support.
privacy.s3L3: Send newsletters and product updates if you have subscribed (you can unsubscribe at any time via the link in any newsletter).
privacy.s3L4: Improve our website, products, and customer service through aggregated analytics.
privacy.s3L5: Comply with legal obligations, including export and tax regulations.
privacy.s3L6: Protect against fraud, abuse, and unauthorized use of our website.
privacy.s4Body: Under the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and the UK GDPR, we rely on the following legal bases to process your personal data: (a) your consent (e.g., newsletter subscription); (b) the performance of a contract or pre-contractual measures at your request (e.g., responding to a quote request); (c) our legitimate interests in operating and improving our B2B website (e.g., aggregate analytics); and (d) compliance with legal obligations.
privacy.s5Title: 5. Third parties we share data with
privacy.s5Intro: We use the following third-party service providers ("processors"). We do not sell your personal data to anyone.
privacy.s5L1: Google Analytics 4 (Google LLC, United States) — aggregate website analytics. IP addresses are anonymized.
privacy.s5L2: Microsoft Clarity (Microsoft Corporation, United States) — heatmaps and anonymized session recordings.
privacy.s5L3: EmailJS (United States) — delivery of contact form submissions to our sales inbox.
privacy.s5L4: Alibaba Cloud Machine Translation (Alibaba Cloud, China) — translation of public website content into German, Spanish, French, and Japanese. No personal data is sent to this service.
privacy.s5L5: Coolify hosting / our infrastructure provider — for serving the website itself.
privacy.s5Outro: These providers process data only on our instructions and are bound by appropriate confidentiality and data-protection terms. We may also disclose information when required by law, court order, or to protect our legal rights.
privacy.s6Body: We use cookies and similar technologies for two purposes: (1) strictly necessary cookies to make the site function correctly (e.g., remembering your selected language); and (2) analytics cookies set by Google Analytics 4 and Microsoft Clarity. You can disable cookies via your browser settings or by using browser-level Do Not Track / Global Privacy Control signals. Disabling cookies will not affect your ability to browse the site or contact us. We do not use advertising or remarketing cookies.
privacy.s7Body: We are based in China. Your data may be processed and stored in China, the United States (Google, Microsoft, EmailJS), and the European Union (depending on your visitor location). For transfers out of the EU/UK, we rely on the European Commission's Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) where applicable.
privacy.s8Title: 8. How long we keep your data
privacy.s8L1: Contact form messages and sales correspondence — retained for up to 5 years for relationship management and tax/export records.
privacy.s8L4: Microsoft Clarity session recordings — typically retained for 90 days.
privacy.s9Intro: Depending on your location, you may have the following rights:
privacy.s9L1: Access — request a copy of the personal data we hold about you.
privacy.s9L2: Rectification — ask us to correct inaccurate or incomplete data.
privacy.s9L3: Erasure — ask us to delete your data ("right to be forgotten"), subject to legal retention requirements.
privacy.s9L4: Restriction or objection — limit or object to certain processing activities.
privacy.s9L5: Data portability — request a machine-readable copy of data you provided to us.
privacy.s9L6: Withdraw consent — at any time, where processing is based on consent (this does not affect prior lawful processing).
privacy.s9L7: Lodge a complaint — with your local data protection authority (in the EU/UK).
privacy.s9Outro: To exercise any of these rights, please email info@xmchichomeware.com. We will respond within 30 days. We may need to verify your identity before processing your request.
privacy.s10Body: Our website and services are intended for business customers and are not directed to children under 16. We do not knowingly collect personal data from children. If you believe we have inadvertently collected data from a minor, please contact us and we will delete it.
privacy.s11Body: We protect your data with appropriate technical and organizational measures: HTTPS/TLS encryption for the website, access controls on our sales systems, and regular review of our hosting and processor security. No method of transmission over the internet is 100% secure, but we work to apply industry-standard protections.
privacy.s12Body: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. When we do, we will revise the "Last updated" date at the top of this page. Significant changes will also be announced via our website or by email to registered users. We encourage you to review this page periodically.
privacy.s13Body: If you have any questions, concerns, or requests regarding this Privacy Policy or your personal data, please contact us at: info@xmchichomeware.com — or by mail at Xiamen Chic Homeware Co., Ltd., 101, No. 8 Houweizhaiding Road, Maluan, Xinglin, Jimei District, Xiamen, Fujian, China.

productContent.overviewWithMaterial: The {title} is a premium {productType} crafted from {materialName}, designed for {app1} and beyond. Whether you're sourcing for {app2} or {app3}, we support full OEM customization including custom size, finish, logo branding, and private label packaging.
productContent.overviewNoMaterial: The {title} is a custom {productType} manufactured by CHIC, a wooden products manufacturer in China specializing in OEM and private label production for global brands. Designed for {app1} and {app2}, this piece is fully customizable — size, material, finish, logo, and packaging — to fit your retail program, gift line, or hospitality account.
productContent.whyChic: CHIC (Xiamen Chic Homeware Co., Ltd.) is a wooden products manufacturer with a 15,000 m² production base in Cao County, Shandong — one of China's most established wooden products clusters. We export to 60+ countries with full OEM and private label support, dedicated quality control, and FBA-ready packaging. Our typical sample lead time is 7–10 days; mass production runs 25–35 days. MOQ starts at 300–500 pcs.

materials.bambooDesc: Bamboo is a dense, fast-growing grass that performs more like a hardwood — naturally water-resistant, antimicrobial, and harder than maple. It regenerates in 3–5 years (vs. 30+ for traditional hardwoods), making it one of the most eco-conscious materials available for kitchenware and home goods.
materials.bambooBestFor: Daily kitchen use, eco-friendly retail lines, gift sets, food-contact applications.
materials.acaciaDesc: Acacia is a premium hardwood with rich, contrasting tiger-stripe grain that ranges from warm gold to deep walnut tones. Its natural oils make it water-resistant and food-safe without heavy chemical finishes. Dense and scratch-resistant — built to handle daily use.
materials.acaciaBestFor: Cheese boards, charcuterie platters, serving trays, premium retail packaging.
materials.walnutDesc: Black walnut is a luxury North American hardwood prized for its rich chocolate-brown color and tight, elegant grain. Its premium feel and timeless appeal make it the top tier choice for high-end gift packaging and collector pieces.
materials.walnutBestFor: Luxury gift boxes, watch boxes, jewelry presentation, premium charcuterie.
materials.pineDesc: Pine is a soft, lightweight wood with prominent knots and a clean, blonde appearance. Its straight grain accepts paint and stain beautifully, making it a versatile choice for farmhouse-style and rustic product lines. The most budget-friendly solid wood option.
materials.pineBestFor: Painted finishes, rustic decor, distressed looks, high-volume retail.
materials.paulowniaDesc: Often called the 'aluminum of timber,' Paulownia is roughly 40% lighter than pine but with a high strength-to-weight ratio. Stable, easy to ship, and naturally resistant to warping — ideal for export-heavy programs where freight cost and dimensional stability matter.
materials.paulowniaBestFor: Storage boxes, drawer organizers, lightweight gift packaging.
materials.oakDesc: Oak is a classic, durable hardwood with strong, open grain and warm honey tones. It develops a beautiful patina over time and is associated with traditional craftsmanship. Heavy, sturdy, and built to last for decades.
materials.oakBestFor: Heritage-style products, premium kitchenware, statement pieces.
materials.beechDesc: Beech is a hard, close-grained European hardwood with a uniform creamy color and subtle figuring. Smooth, easy to machine, and food-safe — a favorite for utensils, cutting boards, and modern minimalist designs.
materials.beechBestFor: Utensils, cutting boards, modern Scandinavian style.
materials.rubberDesc: Rubberwood is a sustainable hardwood harvested from rubber trees after they finish producing latex. Dense, pale, and uniformly grained — it accepts stain readily and offers excellent cost-to-quality ratio for everyday kitchen and storage products.
materials.rubberBestFor: Cutting boards, painted boxes, budget-friendly serving pieces.
materials.teakDesc: Teak is the gold standard for water resistance and durability — naturally oily, dimensionally stable, and rich golden-brown. It outperforms most woods outdoors and is a luxury material for high-end serving and decor pieces.
materials.teakBestFor: Outdoor serving trays, premium kitchenware, hospitality lines.
materials.sapeleDesc: Sapele is a premium African hardwood with a deep red-brown color and shimmering interlocked grain that catches the light. Similar to mahogany but harder and more sustainable — a refined choice for premium presentation pieces.
materials.sapeleBestFor: Luxury gift boxes, watch boxes, presentation cases.
materials.mdfDesc: MDF (medium-density fiberboard) with real wood veneer offers the look of solid wood with superior dimensional stability and lower cost. Ideal when consistency, painted finishes, or large flat panels are needed.
materials.mdfBestFor: Painted boxes, large-format storage, retail display pieces.
materials.plywoodDesc: Plywood — multiple thin layers of wood glued cross-grain — offers excellent strength, stability, and resistance to warping. Versatile, cost-effective, and a workhorse material for structural elements in wooden products.
materials.plywoodBestFor: Structural storage boxes, drawer organizers, large flat panels.

materialGuide.atGlanceIntro: Quick comparison of the materials we work with. Click any wood to jump to its full profile.
materialGuide.wood1Tagline: The all-rounder hardwood for premium kitchenware.
materialGuide.wood1Intro: A durable hardwood with rich, irregular grain — extremely popular for wooden plates, serving trays, cutting boards and kitchen organizers. Excellent food-contact safety with proper finishes.
materialGuide.wood1Adv2: Natural color variation gives a premium look
materialGuide.wood2Tagline: Premium dark hardwood for high-end gifts and luxury packaging.
materialGuide.wood2Intro: A premium hardwood with deep chocolate tones and elegant straight grain. The go-to material when the brand brief says "luxury" — watches, jewelry, premium gift sets and heirloom keepsake boxes.
materialGuide.wood2Adv1: Striking dark color, no staining required
materialGuide.wood2Adv3: Reads as high-end on a retail shelf
materialGuide.wood2Adv4: Pairs beautifully with brass / leather accents
materialGuide.wood3Intro: Technically a grass, but used as a wood. Pale, smooth, sustainable — bamboo is the choice for eco-conscious brands and Amazon sellers selling kitchen storage and organizers.
materialGuide.wood4Tagline: Lightweight, affordable softwood — workhorse for volume orders.
materialGuide.wood4Intro: A pale, lightweight softwood that takes paint and stain beautifully. Best for budget-friendly storage boxes, larger organizers and pieces where weight (and shipping cost) matter.
materialGuide.wood4Adv2: Easy to machine, shape and assemble
materialGuide.wood4Adv3: Accepts paint, stain or natural finish
materialGuide.wood5Tagline: Ultra-lightweight option for big boxes that ship cheap.
materialGuide.wood5Intro: One of the lightest commercial timbers available. Paulownia is the secret weapon for brands shipping large wooden boxes — wine cases, gift packaging, decorative storage — where every kilo of freight cost counts.
materialGuide.wood6Tagline: Classic premium hardwood — built for the long haul.
materialGuide.wood6Intro: A long-favored European hardwood, prized for its open, classic grain and outstanding durability. Used where the product needs to last decades — premium boards, furniture-grade organizers and statement gifts.
materialGuide.wood7Tagline: Smooth, even-grained hardwood — the minimalist's choice.
materialGuide.wood7Intro: A pale European hardwood with a fine, uniform grain. Works beautifully for clean, minimal product designs — Scandinavian-inspired kitchenware and household items where the wood should feel quiet, not loud.
materialGuide.wood8Tagline: Pale, sustainable hardwood reclaimed from rubber plantations.
materialGuide.wood8Intro: After rubber trees stop producing latex, the timber is harvested instead of burned — making rubber wood one of the most sustainable hardwoods on the market. Pale, even and strong — perfect for everyday kitchenware and organizers.
materialGuide.wood8Adv3: Light, even color — easy to brand
materialGuide.wood9Tagline: Naturally water-resistant — premium pick for cutting boards.
materialGuide.wood9Intro: Naturally rich in oils that repel water, teak resists warping and cracking better than almost any other timber. Premium choice for boards, trays and storage that may meet moisture or humidity.
materialGuide.wood10Intro: A tropical hardwood with a warm reddish tone, often used as a cost-effective stand-in for true mahogany. Strong, stable and visually striking — a great fit for plates, trays and decorative pieces.
materialGuide.wood11Tagline: Engineered budget option for painted, printed, or laminated finishes.
materialGuide.wood11Intro: Medium-density fiberboard. Not a solid wood — it's an engineered panel. Smooth, dimensionally stable and cheap. The choice when the design relies on paint, print, decals or laminate rather than visible grain.
materialGuide.wood11Adv1: Perfectly smooth — ideal for paint / print
materialGuide.wood11Adv2: Consistent quality, no knots or splits
materialGuide.wood12Tagline: Layered engineered wood — strong, stable, finish-flexible.
materialGuide.wood12Intro: Multiple veneer layers cross-bonded for strength and stability. Plywood resists warping better than solid wood at the same thickness — a reliable engine for storage boxes and large organizers, often surfaced with a veneer for a wood look at a lower cost.
materialGuide.wood12Adv3: Surface can be veneered, laminated or wrapped
materialGuide.ucIntro: Same product type, different priorities — premium feel vs. shipping cost vs. brand palette. Here's how we usually recommend wood by the job at hand.
materialGuide.uc1Body: Watch boxes, jewelry boxes, luxury gift sets — the wood IS the product.
materialGuide.uc2Body: Knives, juices, frequent washing — needs hardness + moisture resistance.
materialGuide.uc3Body: Wine cases, large gift packaging — every kilo of freight matters.
materialGuide.uc4Body: Brand color, custom artwork, promotional items.
materialGuide.uc6Body: Cost target driven, FBA dimension limits, bulk repeatability.
materialGuide.jankaIntro: Janka hardness measures how much force it takes to embed a small steel ball half-way into the wood. Higher numbers = better resistance to dents, scratches and cutting-board cuts. Anything above ~1,000 lbf is generally suitable for heavy-use kitchenware.
materialGuide.jankaFooter: Indicative reference values — actual results vary by species sub-variety, growing region, and finishing process.
materialGuide.finishIntro: The wood is half the story — the finish decides how the product feels, how it ages, and which compliance standards it meets. Here's what we offer.
materialGuide.finish1Body: Clear protective coat that meets food-contact safety standards. Most common finish for kitchenware.
materialGuide.finish2Body: Natural, food-safe finish that nourishes the wood. Needs periodic re-application.
materialGuide.finish3Body: Soft luster, hand-feel finish that protects against moisture without chemicals.
materialGuide.finish4Body: Hard-wearing glossy or matte coat. Great for decorative pieces — not for cutting boards.
materialGuide.finish5Body: Tinted finish that lets the natural grain show through. Custom colors on request.
materialGuide.finish6Body: Full opaque color. Works best on MDF, pine, paulownia and plywood. Print-ready surface.
materialGuide.finish7Body: Sanded only, no finish — the wood will patina with use. Lowest cost.
materialGuide.brIntro: Every wooden product we ship can carry your brand in some form — from a discreet laser mark to a full-color UV print on every face. Pick the technique that fits your wood, your budget and your shelf positioning.
materialGuide.br1Body: Burned-in mark, dark on light wood, virtually permanent. Best for logos, model numbers, batch IDs.
materialGuide.br2Body: Metallic gold, silver, copper or color foil pressed into the wood with heat. Premium retail look.
materialGuide.br3Body: Pressed indent without ink — subtle, classy, ages well. Reads as luxury.
materialGuide.br4Body: 1–6 spot colors on flat surfaces. Cost-effective for medium runs and bold logos.
materialGuide.br5Body: Full CMYK photo-quality print, including white. Photo, gradient and pattern artwork.
materialGuide.br6Body: Transfers logo onto curved or uneven surfaces — bowls, handles, lid edges.
materialGuide.br7Body: CNC-cut foam, velvet wrap, microfiber lining or molded pulp inserts inside the box.
materialGuide.certIntro: The wood you choose plus the supplier you choose decides which certifications you can claim. Here are the documents we can supply on request — typically issued per-order with the shipment.
materialGuide.cert1Body: Sustainably-sourced wood from certified forests. Required by many EU retailers and brands aiming at FSC consumers.
materialGuide.cert2Body: Restricts hazardous substances in finishes, glues and coatings. Required for products imported into the European Union.
materialGuide.cert3Full: California Air Resources Board (also TSCA Title VI in the US)
materialGuide.cert3Body: Caps formaldehyde emissions in MDF, plywood and particleboard. Mandatory for the US market.
materialGuide.cert4Body: Independent testing that confirms finishes are safe for direct food contact — required for cutting boards, plates, utensils.
materialGuide.cert5Body: Our factory operates under ISO 9001 quality processes — written procedures for sampling, production and outgoing QC.
materialGuide.cert6Body: Available on request — covers labor, health & safety, environment and business ethics. Required by major retailers.
materialGuide.careIntro: Use this on your hangtags, packaging inserts and product pages. Educated end-customers return fewer products and leave better reviews.
materialGuide.care1Body: Dishwashers wreck wooden items — the heat, detergent and prolonged moisture cause warping and cracking. Wash with mild soap and a soft sponge.
materialGuide.care2Body: Don't leave wooden boards or trays sitting in water. Towel-dry right after washing and air-dry standing on edge.
materialGuide.care3Body: Apply food-grade mineral oil or a board butter monthly (more often with heavy use). Wipe on, let sit 15 min, buff off the excess.
materialGuide.care4Body: UV light fades wood color, dry heat causes splits. Keep wooden products away from windows and heaters.
materialGuide.care5Body: Sudden humidity changes cause warping. We pre-condition wood to ~8–12% moisture for export — your end-customers should avoid extreme climate swings.
materialGuide.care6Body: Wooden surfaces can be refreshed at home — light sand with 220-grit and re-oil. Brings back the original look.
materialGuide.ckIntro: Save this list. Send it to any factory you're evaluating — including us. The answers separate real manufacturers from middlemen.
materialGuide.ck1: What is the wood species' Latin name? (E.g. "Acacia mangium" — beware of generic terms like "hardwood")
materialGuide.ck2: Is this a real factory or a trading agent? Can you visit the factory or get a virtual tour?
materialGuide.ck3: Where is the wood actually sourced from? Plantation? Forest? Country of origin?
materialGuide.ck4: What's the moisture content target before assembly? (8–12% is typical for export.)
materialGuide.ck5: Do you provide third-party QC reports — material, dimensional, drop-test?
materialGuide.ck6: Can you supply FSC, REACH, CARB P2 documents on this order?
materialGuide.ck7: What food-safe testing is available — LFGB, FDA migration test, BPA-free?
materialGuide.ck8: How is the wood pre-treated against insects? (Heat, kiln, fumigation?)
materialGuide.ck9: What's the standard finish, and what custom finishes do you support?
materialGuide.ck10: What's the typical lead time for samples vs bulk production?
materialGuide.ck11: What happens if I find defects after delivery — your QC policy and warranty?
materialGuide.faq1Q: What's the difference between hardwood and softwood — and which should I pick?
materialGuide.faq1A: Hardwood comes from broad-leaved trees (acacia, walnut, oak, beech, teak, sapele, rubber, paulownia). Softwood comes from coniferous trees (pine). Hardwoods generally last longer and look more premium, but cost more and are heavier. For cutting boards, jewelry boxes and high-end gifts: hardwood. For large painted boxes, budget storage and shipping-cost-sensitive items: pine or paulownia.
materialGuide.faq2Q: How do you ensure your wooden kitchenware is food-safe?
materialGuide.faq2A: Three layers: the wood itself is sanded and pre-treated with no toxic glues; the finish is a food-grade lacquer, mineral oil or beeswax that meets FDA / LFGB migration limits; and we issue a third-party food-contact safety report per order. For cutting boards we avoid varnishes — those crack with knife use and trap bacteria.
materialGuide.faq3Q: Which wood is best for a heavy-use chef's cutting board?
materialGuide.faq3A: Hardwoods that score 1,200+ on Janka — acacia, beech, and teak are the standard picks. Acacia has the best grain look at a reasonable price. Teak is the most water-resistant. End-grain construction adds another step up in knife-friendliness.
materialGuide.faq4Q: Will the wood warp or crack during international shipping?
materialGuide.faq4A: Properly pre-conditioned wood at 8–12% moisture content, sealed with a stable finish and packed with desiccant in vacuum-sealed master cartons travels reliably. We pre-condition all wood before assembly and document moisture content per batch.
materialGuide.faq5Q: Can I get FSC-certified wood — and does it cost more?
materialGuide.faq5A: Yes for acacia, oak, beech, walnut, sapele and bamboo. FSC-certified material adds roughly 8–15% to the wood cost (not the total product cost) and requires us to issue a Chain-of-Custody certificate per order. Lead time may be slightly longer.
materialGuide.faq6Q: What moisture content should I specify in my purchase order?
materialGuide.faq6A: For export to North America and Western Europe: 8–10%. For humid markets (Southeast Asia, coastal Australia): 10–12%. For very dry destinations (Arizona, Middle East), inform us early — we'll dry the wood lower and add a desiccant pack.
materialGuide.faq7Q: When should I use solid wood vs. plywood / MDF?
materialGuide.faq7A: Solid wood when the wood IS part of the appeal — kitchenware, gift packaging, premium products. Plywood when you need a stable, large flat surface (storage box panels) at lower cost. MDF when the design relies on paint, print or laminate rather than visible grain.
materialGuide.faq8Q: How does grain direction affect product strength?
materialGuide.faq8A: Wood is much stronger along its grain than across it. For cutting boards, "edge-grain" boards have good knife feel; "end-grain" boards are knife-friendliest and most premium-looking. We adjust grain orientation in the CAD stage of every custom project.
materialGuide.bottomCtaTitle: Still not sure which wood is right for your product?
materialGuide.bottomCtaBody: Send us your design, target price and target market. Our engineering team will recommend 1–2 wood options and produce material samples for free.

cases.title: Trusted by brands across 60+ countries
cases.intro: We've worked with buyers from the United States, United Kingdom, Germany, Canada, Japan, and Australia for four years or more. Below are anonymized snapshots of long-term partnerships — what they ordered, how the program evolved, and what they tell us about working with CHIC. We don't publish brand names out of respect for our partners' confidentiality, but we'll happily provide verified references after a signed NDA.
cases.case1Industry: Home storage brand · Amazon FBA seller
cases.case1Products: OEM wooden storage boxes with custom velvet inserts
cases.case1Story: Started with a single drawer-organizer SKU. Over five seasons we co-developed eight SKUs with FBA-ready retail packaging. When tariff costs spiked in 2024, we redesigned the construction (3-layer plywood for non-visible structural parts, solid acacia where the buyer sees it) so the brand could hold its target retail price without a noticeable quality drop.
cases.case1Quote: Chic understands our retail price points and works with us on cost engineering — not just product specs.
cases.case2Products: Wooden tea boxes (4 / 8 / 12 compartment) + matching display stands
cases.case2Story: Started from a generic compartment box and iterated together to a signature design — magnetic closure, acacia-and-walnut two-tone, foil-stamped brand logo, color-printed inserts. We went through six prototype rounds together to find the right lid finish; the brand stuck with us through every revision and the result has become their flagship retail product.
cases.case2Quote: Always responsive, always solid quality. Six iterations on one lid — they never lost patience.
cases.case3Industry: Mid-market kitchenware brand · German retail chains
cases.case3Products: Acacia cheese boards, serving trays, magnetic knife blocks
cases.case3Story: Continuous refinement on food-safe coating, handle ergonomics, and EU-compliant packaging marks. When German retail margins tightened in 2024, we proposed a paulownia-core + acacia-veneer hybrid for the serving tray line that cut material cost by ~30% with no visible quality drop on the user-facing surfaces. The brand still has every original SKU in their catalog.
cases.case3Quote: Reliable QC and proactive cost engineering. We treat them like an internal team — not a remote vendor.
cases.case4Products: Wooden gift boxes, keepsake boxes, corporate gift programs
cases.case4Story: Highly seasonal — about 90% of the year's volume ships between August and October. We built a production-buffer agreement so peak-season surges don't trigger rush surcharges or quality compromises. Across four consecutive holiday seasons we've hit every delivery deadline, which is critical for their year-end revenue.
cases.case4Quote: Four holiday seasons in a row, never a missed deadline. That's the difference for a seasonal business.
cases.case5Industry: Premium retail · home goods specialty store
cases.case5Products: Acacia serving trays, sofa trays, premium gift sets
cases.case5Story: Strict Japanese retail quality standards. We adapted our QC process specifically for this account — 100% visual inspection (vs. our standard AQL sampling), per-batch moisture-content verification, and special inner packaging to meet Japanese retail receiving standards. Five years in, zero quality rejections on shipment.
cases.case5Quote: Their quality consistently meets Japanese retail standards — we don't have to inspect at our warehouse.
cases.case6Products: Acacia serving trays, cutting boards, hospitality-grade
cases.case6Story: Bulk hospitality orders with tight unit economics. Helped them standardize on a 3-SKU core lineup that covers 90% of their restaurant customer base, which simplified their warehousing and reduced their working-capital tie-up by ~40%. We hold stock on the three top sellers so they can replenish in two weeks rather than two months.
cases.case6Quote: Stock-on-hand on our top three SKUs is a small thing that completely changed our cash flow.
cases.ctaTitle: Want to be our next long-term partner?
cases.ctaBody: Most of our buyers start with one product, one sample, and a single conversation. Tell us what you're trying to source and we'll come back within one business day with a real proposal.
cases.disclaimer: Case studies are anonymized to protect partner confidentiality. Specific volumes, SKU counts, and years reflect typical engagement patterns and may vary by account. References available after a signed NDA.

team.metaTitle: Meet the Team — CHIC Wooden Expert
team.metaDesc: The people behind CHIC: founder Chuan Pu, sales lead Cassie Zhang, production lead Yucheng Lu, design lead Vivi Cheng, and quality lead Lingling Zhang.
team.pageIntro: CHIC is a real factory run by real people. Below are the five who lead our day-to-day work — from the founder who set up our manufacturing base back in 2017, to the team members you'll be talking to and emailing once you start a project with us.
team.chuanBio: Chuan Pu founded the CHIC factory in 2017 after years working across China's wooden-products supply chain — from raw timber sourcing in Cao County to finished export programs for global brands. In 2020 he registered Xiamen Chic Homeware Co., Ltd. to take the company's products direct-to-export. His hands-on background informs how CHIC approaches material selection and helps buyers match the right wood to the right project. Chuan believes that better-informed buyers make better products, and that sustainably sourced wood, well crafted, will outperform a generic hardwood every time.
team.cassieBio: Cassie leads the sales team and is the first point of contact for most of our long-term partners. With many years in foreign trade and B2B export, she is known for clear communication, fast turnaround on quotes and samples, and a no-shortcuts approach to problem-solving — whether that means untangling a sudden specification change mid-production or coordinating an urgent FBA-deadline shipment. Her view: the supplier who takes ownership when something goes wrong is the supplier worth keeping.
team.yuchengBio: Yucheng runs day-to-day production at our 15,000 m² facility in Cao County. After three years at CHIC he knows every machine, every finisher, and every workshop lead by name. His job is to translate signed sales orders into finished goods on the dock — on spec, on time, and at the quality level the buyer signed off on. When sales says 'the buyer wants tighter grain on the lid,' Yucheng is the person who makes that happen on the floor.
team.viviBio: Vivi is the bridge between buyer intent and what the factory floor can actually build. Once a product brief is confirmed with sales, she develops the construction drawings, material specifications, and prototype iterations — coordinating directly with the production team and the sales account manager. Her focus: drawings the workshop can hit, finishes the buyer will love, and details that survive a 30,000-piece production run without drift.
team.linglingBio: Lingling owns quality from in-process inspection through final pre-shipment QC. She runs the 100% visual inspection for premium accounts and AQL sampling for the rest, and writes the corrective-action reports when a sample fails. Her standing rule on the floor: if a defect is found, it gets logged, root-caused, and fixed — never quietly shipped. That is how buyers come back season after season.

founderStory.title: Why I started CHIC.
founderStory.p1: I started CHIC after a decade in wooden products because I kept seeing the same problem on both sides of the table.
founderStory.p2: Many of our buyers — brilliant product designers, established kitchenware brands, talented Amazon sellers — didn't fully understand the trade-offs between different wood materials. They would specify walnut for a budget-line storage box, or pine for a premium serving tray, and the result was either over-engineered cost or under-delivered quality.
founderStory.p3: On the factory side, suppliers often just took the order without flagging the mismatch. Easier to quote what the buyer asked for than to explain why a different wood would actually work better.
founderStory.p4: CHIC was built to close that gap. We treat material education as part of our service — we will tell you when paulownia makes more sense than pine, or when an acacia-walnut two-tone delivers more visual value than solid walnut at the same retail price. We invest in responsible sourcing and we publish a detailed material guide because better-informed buyers make better products and waste less wood doing it.
founderStory.p5: Nearly a decade into running our own factory, that is still the work: matching the right wood to the right project, making it well, and being honest about what each option can and cannot do.

timeline.title: From factory floor to global B2B partner.
timeline.intro: Built step by step over almost a decade — each milestone reflects the work of the team, our partners, and a continuing commitment to making better wooden products.

timeline.m2Body: Xiamen Chic Homeware Co., Ltd. is registered to handle export business directly. This unlocks shipping to the United States, EU, UK, Japan, and Australia under our own brand and as OEM for global partners.
timeline.m3Body: Year-end results cross 20,000,000 RMB (~2.8M USD) in export sales — driven by repeat orders from 4+ year B2B partners across six countries.
timeline.m4Body: CHIC is verified as an Alibaba Gold Supplier — a public, third-party trust signal for international buyers sourcing from our online catalog.
timeline.m5Body: With expanded production capacity, additional OEM programs, and a new multilingual B2B website (this one), the team is on track to roughly double annual revenue versus 2025.

about.faq1Q: What kind of products do you manufacture?
about.faq1A: We specialize in designing and manufacturing high-quality wooden organizers and premium packaging. Our main product lines include: wooden desk & office organizers (pen holders, monitor stands, document trays), wooden beauty & cosmetic organizers (jewelry boxes, vanity trays, brush holders), kitchen & dining wooden storage (knife blocks, utensil caddies, spice boxes, bread boxes), home storage organizers (countertop, pantry, entryway, sofa trays), and gift / retail packaging (watch boxes, tea & coffee boxes, wine boxes, custom presentation cases).
about.faq2Q: Are you a real factory or a trading company?
about.faq2A: We are a real wooden products manufacturer. Our production base is in Cao County, Shandong Province — one of China's most established wooden homeware hubs. Our sales and design office is in Xiamen, Fujian. You are welcome to visit either site, or take a virtual factory tour over video call.
about.faq3Q: What is your typical MOQ and lead time?
about.faq3A: MOQ varies by product complexity. As a guide: 100 pcs for simple designs and samples, 500 pcs for production runs. Sample lead time: 7–10 days for standard samples, 15–25 days for custom-tooled samples. Production lead time: 25–45 days after sample confirmation, depending on order quantity, materials and surface treatment.
about.faq4Q: Which countries do you export to?
about.faq4A: Our wooden products are exported to 60+ countries, with the largest customer bases in the United States, Canada, the United Kingdom, Germany, France, Italy, Japan, South Korea and Australia. We have hands-on experience with Amazon FBA logistics, retail-chain compliance and door-to-door delivery (DDP).
about.faq5Q: What customization options do you support?
about.faq5A: Material (12+ wood types), size, structure, surface finish, color, logo (laser, foil, deboss, screen print, UV print), inserts (foam, velvet, microfiber), packaging (color box, kraft, gift, FBA-ready cartons), and hardware (hinges, locks, handles, magnetic closure). about.faq6Q: Do you offer FSC, REACH or food-safety certifications?
about.faq6A: Yes. We can source FSC-certified wood, use REACH-compliant finishes, supply CARB P2 / TSCA Title VI compliant engineered panels, and provide LFGB / FDA food-contact safety reports. Compliance documents are issued per order with the shipment.
about.faq7Q: How does the sampling and ordering process work?
about.faq7A: Send us your brief → we quote within 24 hours → on PI confirmation we develop a sample (paid, refundable on bulk) → photos and physical sample shipped for approval → on sample sign-off we receive the deposit and start production → final QC and pre-shipment inspection → balance payment and shipment. Each stage has a clear timeline and contact point.
about.faq8Q: What payment terms do you accept?
about.faq8A: Standard terms are 30% T/T deposit, 70% balance before shipment. For repeat customers and larger orders we also work with L/C at sight, OA terms, and via Alibaba Trade Assurance. We accept USD, EUR and CNY.
about.bottomCtaTitle: Have a brief? Let's build it together.
about.bottomCtaBody: Send your specs, sketches or reference samples — we typically reply within 24 hours with a quote and a sample timeline.

woodenStashBox.faq6Q: Are wooden stash boxes solid wood or veneered?
woodenStashBox.faq6A: We build in solid, kiln-dried wood by default. Veneer over MDF is available where you need large flat panels or lower cost, but we'll tell you exactly what you're getting — no chipboard sold as “solid wood”.
woodenStashBox.faq7Q: Do certain woods affect the smell or flavour of the contents?
woodenStashBox.faq7A: Strongly aromatic woods (like cedar) can impart scent, which some buyers want and others don't. For neutral storage we recommend low-odour woods such as acacia, walnut, beech or pine with food-safe finishes.
woodenStashBox.faq8Q: What lock options are most reliable?
woodenStashBox.faq8A: Barrel locks and quality clasp or magnetic closures are the most durable. Combination dials are popular but fail more often, so we use better-grade hardware and can advise per use case.

giftBox.faq3Q: Can you make fitted inserts for my product?
giftBox.faq3A: Yes. We die-cut foam, EVA, fabric-wrapped or paper inserts to your exact product dimensions so it sits securely and presents well on opening.
giftBox.faq4Q: What branding methods do you offer?
giftBox.faq4A: Laser engraving, UV printing, hot foil stamping, screen printing and embossed metal plates. Engraving is durable and included on most projects; colour printing and foil are quoted per design.
giftBox.faq5Q: Which woods and finishes are available?
giftBox.faq5A: Acacia, walnut, bamboo and pine are most popular, with food-safe, low-odour finishes. Walnut and acacia suit premium gifts; bamboo and pine keep costs down at volume.
giftBox.faq6Q: Are the boxes solid wood or veneered?
giftBox.faq6A: We build in solid, kiln-dried wood by default. Veneer over MDF is available for large flat panels or lower cost, but we'll tell you exactly what you're getting — no chipboard sold as “solid wood”.
giftBox.faq7Q: Can the box be reused after the gift?
giftBox.faq7A: Yes — that's a key advantage of wood over card. A well-made wooden gift box is kept and reused for storage, extending your brand's visibility long after purchase.
giftBox.faq8Q: Is the packaging retail and Amazon FBA ready?
giftBox.faq8A: Yes. We prepare retail-ready and FBA-compliant outer packaging with correct sizing, labelling and drop-test-worthy protection so boxes arrive undamaged.
giftBox.faq9Q: Do you offer FSC-certified wood and food-safe finishes?
giftBox.faq9A: Yes. We can supply FSC chain-of-custody certified wood on request and use food-safe, low-odour finishes suitable for gourmet and confectionery gifting.
giftBox.bottomCtaTitle: Get a quote for your custom wooden gift box
giftBox.bottomCtaBody: Send your design, dimensions and target quantity — we'll come back with pricing, material and insert options, and a sample timeline within one business day.
giftBox.lidTitle: Which lid style fits your gift box?
giftBox.lidIntro: The lid is the first thing a recipient touches. Here's how the main mechanisms compare so you can match feel, use and budget.
giftBox.lid1Feel: Premium snap-close, seamless front panel, great for unboxing video
giftBox.lid1Best: Cosmetics, spirits, gift sets, premium retail
giftBox.lid3Feel: Timeless, heirloom feel, opens without removing the lid
giftBox.lid4Feel: Tactile pull-out reveal, ideal for layered or multi-piece sets
giftBox.brandTitle: How your logo goes on the box
giftBox.brandIntro: We offer every common branding method. This is how they compare on look, colour and ideal use.
giftBox.brand1Effect: Permanent etched mark with natural burnt tone
giftBox.brand1Best: Durable logos on most projects (often included)
giftBox.brand2Effect: Sharp digital print directly on wood
giftBox.brand5Effect: Raised branded plate fixed to the box

giftBox.costTitle: What drives the price of a wooden gift box
giftBox.costIntro: Unit cost depends mostly on these factors. Tell us your priorities and we'll find the best balance of look and budget.
giftBox.cost1Body: Solid hardwood (walnut, acacia) costs more than pine, bamboo or MDF-with-veneer; joinery and wall thickness add too.
giftBox.cost2Body: Magnets, brass clasps and hinges add cost; sliding and lift-off lids are the most economical.
giftBox.cost3Body: Die-cut foam, fabric-wrapped trays and velvet linings raise both perceived value and unit price.
giftBox.cost4Body: Engraving is usually included; UV colour, hot foil and embossed plates add tooling or per-piece cost.
giftBox.cost5Body: Higher volumes cut the per-unit price by spreading tooling, setup and print costs — the biggest lever.
giftBox.effTitle: See your logo on real boxes
giftBox.effIntro: Laser engraving doesn't add ink — it burns the wood surface, so the same logo looks different on every material. Below are real samples from our factory, plus UV printing and foil for when you need colour or shine.
giftBox.effLaserNote: Because engraving relies on the wood's own colour and density, paler, even-grained woods give the crispest, highest-contrast mark, while darker or stronger-grained woods give a subtler, more tonal result. Here is the same logo on four common woods:
giftBox.eff1Body: Pale, even and dense — bamboo burns to a clean medium-brown with sharp edges and excellent contrast, ideal for fine detail and small text.
giftBox.eff2Body: Rich, varied grain and a warm mid-tone — engraving reads as a deep brown, with the natural grain adding a premium, handcrafted character (contrast shifts slightly across the grain).
giftBox.eff3Body: Very light and soft — paulownia engraves easily and shows a clear light-to-medium brown mark; great for larger logos and a rustic, natural look.
giftBox.eff4Body: On walnut or dark-stained boxes the burn is closer in tone to the surface, giving an understated, elegant low-contrast mark — for maximum pop on dark wood, pair it with foil or a metal plate.
giftBox.effUvTitle: UV printing — full colour, photographic detail
giftBox.effUvBody: UV printing lays a crisp, full-colour image directly onto the wood, so you can reproduce logos with multiple colours, gradients or even photos exactly as designed. The grain often shows subtly through lighter areas, keeping a natural wooden feel while giving brand-accurate colour. Best when your logo isn't a single colour.
giftBox.effGoldTitle: Gold & metallic — foil and plates for luxury
giftBox.effGoldBody: For a premium, high-shine finish we use hot-foil stamping or an embossed metal plate in gold, silver or other metallics. Gold especially stands out on dark wood and dark labels, signalling luxury for gifting, spirits and high-end retail — the look of the finest presentation boxes.
giftBox.faq10Q: Do you make magnetic-closure wooden gift boxes?
giftBox.faq10A: Yes — magnetic flip-lid (magnetic closure) wooden gift boxes are one of our most popular formats, with hidden magnets for a clean, premium close. We also make sliding, hinged, lift-off and drawer styles.
giftBox.faq11Q: Can I order personalized wooden gift boxes with my logo in bulk?
giftBox.faq11A: Yes. We produce personalized and custom-logo wooden gift boxes wholesale, with laser engraving, UV printing or hot foil. Bulk orders lower the unit price; MOQs typically start at 300–500 units.
giftBox.faq12Q: What wooden gift boxes suit wine, candles, cosmetics or jewellery?
giftBox.faq12A: We build wooden gift and packaging boxes for wine and spirits, candles, cosmetics, jewellery, tea and gourmet — each with a fitted insert sized to the product. Tell us the item and we'll recommend the format.
giftBox.faq13Q: Do you offer small or low-MOQ wooden gift boxes wholesale?
giftBox.faq13A: Yes. We offer small and standard sizes at wholesale with realistic MOQs — simple slide-lid boxes can start lower than fully custom magnetic or lined boxes. Share your size and quantity for an exact quote.
